MIMIC-IV: Adult ICU stay; first eligible support stay per patient
eICU: Adult ICU stay; first eligible support stay per patient
SICdb: Adult ICU case; first eligible support case per patient
Harmonized_rule_or_direction: One index support episode per patient/case was retained for the primary analysis.
All windows were anchored to the first qualifying early support time.
Baseline physiology was summarized before the response window.
Observed recovery was measured before the 24 h landmark.
Primary outcomes were evaluated after the landmark.
Higher response minus baseline indicates better pressure recovery.

eICU: Harmonized vasoactive burden from medication records
SICdb: Harmonized vasoactive burden / agent burden from treatment records
Harmonized_rule_or_direction: Lower response burden relative to baseline indicates better support de-escalation; database-specific raw units were not directly compared.
Higher response relative to baseline indicates better renal/perfusion recovery.
Creatinine worsening was used for secondary organ nonrecovery analyses, not the core HRC construct.
Lactate nonclearance and hyperlactatemia were secondary endpoints due variable availability.

eICU: Acute Physiology Score and APACHE score
Harmonized_rule_or_direction: Severity was harmonized by role rather than identical raw scale.
Used for expected-recovery and outcome adjustment where available.
Deaths before 24 h were handled in landmark sensitivity analyses.

MIMIC-IV: Persistent vasoactive support, oliguria, creatinine worsening, lactate nonclearance/hyperlactatemia, RRT/ventilation modules where available
eICU: Persistent vasoactive support, oliguria, creatinine worsening, lactate nonclearance/hyperlactatemia
SICdb: Persistent vasoactive support proxy, oliguria, creatinine worsening, lactate nonclearance/hyperlactatemia
Harmonized_rule_or_direction: Domain definitions preserved physiologic directionality across databases.

MIMIC-IV: 0-24 h net fluid balance where available
SICdb: 0-24 h fluid exposure rate; net balance not consistently available
Harmonized_rule_or_direction: Fluid adjustment tested whether HRC associations were explained by fluid exposure.

MIMIC-IV: CVP and mean perfusion pressure available in a subset
eICU: Not used for primary cross-database validation
SICdb: Not used for primary cross-database validation
Harmonized_rule_or_direction: Used only for MIMIC-IV mechanistic validation because cross-database availability was insufficient.

MIMIC-IV: IABP, Impella, ECMO from procedure/device records
eICU: Not primary due inconsistent device capture
SICdb: Not primary due inconsistent device capture
Harmonized_rule_or_direction: MCS analyses were mechanistic, not part of the cross-database HRC definition.
Cluster-aware and database-stratified analyses were used as robustness checks.
